visa_types.name: Affidavit by a parent when applying for a child's Passport in India
visa_types.processing_time:
Strictly do not finalize travel plans before receiving the service applied for. Processing times below are average processing times. Please plan your travel accordingly.
VFS Global/Government of India would not be responsible for any inconvenience/problems faced as a result of finalizing travel plans before obtaining the relevant service.
Applicants sending their documents by Post/Courier are requested to ensure that they are completed and meet requirements. Processing times mentioned do not include transit time by post/courier. Transit times by Courier are generally 1-3 days on average and may be higher in remote or rural areas.
Incomplete applications will lead to an increase in processing time and applicants will need to comply with any changes in the procedure that may occur during this time. As a part of Processing Incomplete applications are returned and Courier Return fees will apply. See terms and conditions for further details
Average Processing Time –
5 days working days
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview

## Who Can Apply?
This affidavit is required when parent(s) is/are applying for their child's passport in India and the other parent(s) is/are in Australia. The parent in Australia has to certify that he/she has no objection to a passport being issued to him/her child in India/overseas.
If parent(s) is/are Australian, the affidavit form needs to be apostilled by DFAT before submitting to VFS centres

## Required Documents
## 
All the below listed required form & document requested are mandatory to be submitted to the ICAC for the application processing.

[inr > aus > en > Consular Services > Affidavit by a parent when applying for a child's Passport in India > Checklist and Application Forms: table2]
All applicants applying for Consular Services are requested to ensure that the correct Indian Mission is selected based on their residential state. Selecting an incorrect mission will require you to fill out a new application form.
- Before submitting your online application, please refer to the jurisdiction table below and confirm that your application is mapped to the correct Indian Mission.
- If the mission selected does not match your residential state, please create a new username and password and complete a fresh application with the correct mapping.
- After completing your application, you may submit it in person by appointment or by post/courier (except OCI applications) to the ICAC’s centres listed for your state.

[inr > aus > en > Consular Services > Checklist and Documents Required: Table]

In Person- If you decide to submit your application in person by appointment at an Application Centre, click here (https://vfs-canada.vercel.app/aus/en/inr/book-an-appointment) to book your appointment.
By Post/ Courier- If you decide to send in your application by Post/ Courier click here (https://services.vfsglobal.com/aus/en/ind/attend-centre) for details on how to submit your application.

## Fees and Charges
FEE CALCULATOR (https://www.vfsvisaservice.com/IHCAUSOnline/FeeCalculator.aspx)
Please click on the Fee Calculator Icon to calculate fees applicable. Fees are subject to revision every month depending upon the prevailing Rate of Exchange.

## Payment Instructions

ICAC Service Fee: Mandatory and payable online at the time of appointment booking.
Government Fees & Levies: Payable only through the approved methods listed below.
Accepted Payment Modes:
EFTPOS / Credit Card (MasterCard or Visa issued in Australia) – for in-person submissions only
Money / Postal Order payable to “VFS Services Australia Pty Ltd”
Cash Policy: All centres operate on a cashless basis; cash payments are not accepted.
For applications sent by Post/Courier to the ICACs:
- Fees can be paid by Money Order/Postal Order in favour of “VFS Services Australia Pty Ltd”
Photocopying, photographs, form filling (by applicant utilising the VAC internet Kiosk), and courier services are provided to applicants without any additional charge.
The prescribed VFS Service Fee is inclusive of these Application Facilitating Services, and no separate amount is payable by applicants

## Processing Time
Strictly do not finalize travel plans before receiving the service applied for. Processing times below are average processing times. Please plan your travel accordingly.
VFS Global/Government of India would not be responsible for any inconvenience/problems faced as a result of finalizing travel plans before obtaining the relevant service.
Applicants sending their documents by Post/Courier are requested to ensure that they are completed and meet requirements. Processing times mentioned do not include transit time by post/courier. Transit times by Courier are generally 1-3 days on average and may be higher in remote or rural areas.
Incomplete applications will lead to an increase in processing time and applicants will need to comply with any changes in the procedure that may occur during this time. As a part of Processing Incomplete applications are returned and Courier Return fees will apply. See terms and conditions for further details
Average Processing Time –
5 days working days

## Photo Specification
Guidelines for ICAO-Compliant Photographs (Consular Applications)
Photographs submitted for passport services must comply with ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization) standards. Applicants are required to ensure that the photograph meets the specifications below at the time of capture or upload.
1. Photo Specifications
- The photograph must be in colour.
- Recommended dimensions: 630 x 810 pixels.
- The image must be clear, sharp, and unedited (no digital alterations or enhancements).
- The background must be plain white.
- The face (head and shoulders) must occupy approximately 80–85% of the frame.
2. Composition & Positioning
- The photograph must show a full face, front view.
- The applicant must be looking directly at the camera.
- The head should be centred within the frame and not tilted.
- The image must include the full head, from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin.
- The photograph should be taken from an appropriate distance (approximately 1.5 metres) to avoid distortion or cropping issues.
3. Facial Features & Expression
- The applicant must have a neutral expression.
- The mouth must be closed.
- Eyes must be open and clearly visible.
- Hair should not cover the eyes or face.
4. Lighting & Image Quality
- Lighting must be uniform and well-balanced.
- There must be no shadows on the face or background.
- Avoid reflections from flash or glasses.
- Red-eye effect must not be present.
- Brightness and contrast must reflect natural skin tones.
- The photograph must not be blurred.
5. Glasses & Accessories
- Glasses should be removed to avoid glare or reflections.
- There must be no distracting objects or shadows in the background.
6. Head Coverings
- Head coverings are not permitted, except for religious reasons.
- In such cases, the following must be clearly visible: 
- Full face from bottom of chin to top of forehead
- Both edges (sides) of the face

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview

## Who Can Apply?
Indian Drivers Licence Verification service is available for Indian nationals only.
The Indian Driver Licence Verification services are not available under the jurisdiction of Consulate General of India, in Melbourne (VIC & TAS).
The Licence Verification Certificate will only be issued in the exact full name as per your driver’s Licence. Ensure when completing the online application the full name on your Online form and on your Driver’s Licence is exactly the same.
If the Name on your Licence is different from your current valid Passport, review the checklist for the procedure you need to complete required by the High Commission of India.
Father’s Name on the Online Application form should be entered exactly as it appears on the Licence.
To ensure your application can be processed, kindly ensure all documents are provided as stated in the checklist. Have all your documents checked as per the checklist and place them in the order as required on the checklist.
If the checklist is not followed, this may result in an incomplete application which cannot be accepted by the High Commission of India for processing.
Important Note:
- There are some Road Transport Authorities in Australia which do not accept this IDLV certification. The applicants may like to check with their local RTA of such details; or something to this effect.
- The verification letters being submitted by IDLV applicants from the concerned RTO/DTO/Licensing authority should preferably on the letterhead with office address, tel/fax number, email address, name and designation of the signatory.
- Submission of fake documents to obtain passport/consular services is a crime and can lead to punitive action by the High Commission/Consulates.
- In such cases wherever a reference has been made to concerned authorities in India, issuance of IDLV is strictly subject to receipt of clearance and thus the indicated processing time is not applicable.
Important Note for Applicants under the jurisdiction of Consulate General of India, in Melbourne (VIC & TAS):
A new process has been put in place for Indian driver licence holders seeking driver licence from VicRoads on the basis of their Indian licences.
Ordinarily, VicRoads would not ask for the Indian Driver Licence Verification (IDLV) certificate issued by the Indian Consulate at Melbourne (CGI Melbourne), for issue of licences to the applicants holding Indian Drivers Licence.
VicRoads would however seek the verification certificate in cases of doubt regarding the authenticity of the (Indian) license. Such cases would be referred to CGI Melbourne for detailed verification for issue of IDLV certificate.
Hence a routine request for IDLV certificates may not be made/filed with CGI/VFS Melbourne.
The process for seeking IDLV for CGI Melbourne, after VicRoads has sought such a certificate from the applicant, remains the same.

## Required Documents
## 
All the below listed required form & document requested are mandatory to be submitted to the ICAC for the application processing.

[inr > aus > en > Consular Services > India Driver Licence Verification (IDLV) > Checklist and Application Forms: Table]

All applicants applying for Consular Services are requested to ensure that the correct Indian Mission is selected based on their residential state. Selecting an incorrect mission will require you to fill out a new application form.
- Before submitting your online application, please refer to the jurisdiction table below and confirm that your application is mapped to the correct Indian Mission.
- If the mission selected does not match your residential state, please create a new username and password and complete a fresh application with the correct mapping.
- After completing your application, you may submit it in person by appointment or by post/courier (except OCI applications) to the ICAC’s centres listed for your state.

[inr > aus > en > Consular Services > Checklist and Documents Required: Table]

In Person- If you decide to submit your application in person by appointment at an Application Centre, click here (https://vfs-canada.vercel.app/aus/en/inr/book-an-appointment) to book your appointment.
By Post/ Courier- If you decide to send in your application by Post/ Courier click here (https://services.vfsglobal.com/aus/en/ind/attend-centre) for details on how to submit your application.

## Fees and Charges

Please click on the Fee Calculator Icon to calculate fees applicable. Fees are subject to revision every month depending upon the prevailing Rate of Exchange.

EFTPOS / Credit Card (MasterCard or Visa issued in Australia) – for in-person submissions only
Money / Postal Order payable to “VFS Services Australia Pty Ltd”
Cash Policy: All centres operate on a cashless basis; cash payments are not accepted.
For applications sent by Post/Courier to the ICACs:
- Fees can be paid by Money Order/Postal Order in favour of “VFS Services Australia Pty Ltd”
Photocopying, photographs, form filling (by applicant utilising the VAC internet Kiosk), and courier services are provided to applicants without any additional charge.
The prescribed VFS Service Fee is inclusive of these Application Facilitating Services, and no separate amount is payable by applicants

## Processing Time
Strictly do not finalize travel plans before receiving the service applied for. Processing times below are average processing times. Please plan your travel accordingly.

Incomplete applications will lead to an increase in processing time and applicants will need to comply with any changes in the procedure that may occur during this time. As a part of Processing Incomplete applications are returned and Courier Return fees will apply. See terms and conditions for further details
Average Processing Time –

## Photo Specification
Guidelines for ICAO-Compliant Photographs (Consular Applications)
Photographs submitted for passport services must comply with ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization) standards. Applicants are required to ensure that the photograph meets the specifications below at the time of capture or upload.
1. Photo Specifications

- The image must be clear, sharp, and unedited (no digital alterations or enhancements).
- The background must be plain white.

2. Composition & Positioning

- The applicant must be looking directly at the camera.
- The head should be centred within the frame and not tilted.
- The image must include the full head, from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin.
- The photograph should be taken from an appropriate distance (approximately 1.5 metres) to avoid distortion or cropping issues.

As a part of Processing Incomplete applications are returned and Courier Return fees will apply. See terms and conditions for further details
Average Processing Time –
5 days working days

## Photo Specification
Guidelines for ICAO-Compliant Photographs (Consular Applications)
Photographs submitted for passport services must comply with ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization) standards. Applicants are required to ensure that the photograph meets the specifications below at the time of capture or upload.
1. Photo Specifications
- The photograph must be in colour.
- Recommended dimensions: 630 x 810 pixels.
- The image must be clear, sharp, and unedited (no digital alterations or enhancements).
- The background must be plain white.
- The face (head and shoulders) must occupy approximately 80–85% of the frame.
2. Composition & Positioning
- The photograph must show a full face, front view.
- The applicant must be looking directly at the camera.
- The head should be centred within the frame and not tilted.
- The image must include the full head, from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin.
- The photograph should be taken from an appropriate distance (approximately 1.5 metres) to avoid distortion or cropping issues.
3. Facial Features & Expression
- The applicant must have a neutral expression.
- The mouth must be closed.
- Eyes must be open and clearly visible.
- Hair should not cover the eyes or face.
4. Lighting & Image Quality
- Lighting must be uniform and well-balanced.
- There must be no shadows on the face or background.
- Avoid reflections from flash or glasses.
- Red-eye effect must not be present.
- Brightness and contrast must reflect natural skin tones.
- The photograph must not be blurred.
5. Glasses & Accessories
- Glasses should be removed to avoid glare or reflections.
- There must be no distracting objects or shadows in the background.
6. Head Coverings
- Head coverings are not permitted, except for religious reasons.
- In such cases, the following must be clearly visible: 
- Full face from bottom of chin to top of forehead
- Both edges (sides) of the face

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview

## Who Can Apply?
Through this service, applicant can attest Civil documents, Property documents, Commercial documents from companies exporting to India, Commercial documents from companies exporting from India, Documents of Foreign nationals (Civil and Property documents) and Power of attorney.
All applicants should ensure that documents submitted for attestation (other than documents issued by Indian authorities) have the apostille stamp from the Australian Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT) before submitting them to VFS centres for attestation.
In some cases DFAT requires that the documents be notarised before they can stamp it, and they may have other requirements; you are advised to check with DFAT before presenting the documents to them.
All Education degrees / documents of India must be first attested from competent authority in India particularly from Consular section of the CPV (Consular Passport Visa) Division, MEA, Patiala House, New Delhi and thereafter can be attested in HCI, Canberra.
Note:
- Wherever the documents are already apostilled by the Australian Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT), no further attestation by the High Commission/Consulates is required as per provisions of The Hague Apostille Convention and such apostilled documents are entitled to recognition in India
- If the applicant, for whatever reason, desires an additional attestation by the High Commission/Consulates, an undertaking letter would need to be given by the applicant that he/she is aware of the provisions of the Apostille Convention and that he/she would still like to have an additional attestation.
Important:
Attestation of signature - This service is given to Indian nationals holding valid Indian Passports only. The signature needs to be first attested by a JP/Notary. In the case of foreign nationals including Australian citizen, the signature should have been apostilled by DFAT before submission to VFS.

## Required Documents
All the below listed required form & document requested are mandatory to be submitted to the ICAC for the application processing.

[inr > aus > en > Consular Services > Attestation of Documents > Checklist and Application Forms: Table]

All applicants applying for Consular Services are requested to ensure that the correct Indian Mission is selected based on their residential state. Selecting an incorrect mission will require you to fill out a new application form.

- After completing your application, you may submit it in person by appointment or by post/courier (except OCI applications) to the ICAC’s centres listed for your state.

[inr > aus > en > Consular Services > Checklist and Documents Required: Table]

In Person- If you decide to submit your application in person by appointment at an Application Centre, click here (https://vfs-canada.vercel.app/aus/en/inr/book-an-appointment) below to book your appointment.
By Post/ Courier- If you decide to send in your application by Post/ Courier click here (https://services.vfsglobal.com/aus/en/ind/attend-centre) for details on how to submit your application.

## Fees and Charges
FEE CALCULATOR (https://www.vfsvisaservice.com/IHCAUSOnline/FeeCalculator.aspx)
Please click on the Fee Calculator Icon to calculate fees applicable. Fees are subject to revision every month depending upon the prevailing Rate of Exchange.

## Payment Instructions

ICAC Service Fee: Mandatory and payable online at the time of appointment booking.
Government Fees & Levies: Payable only through the approved methods listed below.
Accepted Payment Modes:
EFTPOS / Credit Card (MasterCard or Visa issued in Australia) – for in-person submissions only
Money / Postal Order payable to “VFS Services Australia Pty Ltd”
Cash Policy: All centres operate on a cashless basis; cash payments are not accepted.
For applications sent by Post/Courier to the ICACs:
- Fees can be paid by Money Order/Postal Order in favour of “VFS Services Australia Pty Ltd”
Photocopying, photographs, form filling (by applicant utilising the VAC internet Kiosk), and courier services are provided to applicants without any additional charge.
The prescribed VFS Service Fee is inclusive of these Application Facilitating Services, and no separate amount is payable by applicants

## Processing Time
Strictly do not finalize travel plans before receiving the service applied for. Processing times below are average processing times. Please plan your travel accordingly.
VFS Global/Government of India would not be responsible for any inconvenience/problems faced as a result of finalizing travel plans before obtaining the relevant service.
Applicants sending their documents by Post/Courier are requested to ensure that they are completed and meet requirements. Processing times mentioned do not include transit time by post/courier. Transit times by Courier are generally 1-3 days on average and may be higher in remote or rural areas.
Incomplete applications will lead to an increase in processing time and applicants will need to comply with any changes in the procedure that may occur during this time. As a part of Processing Incomplete applications are returned and Courier Return fees will apply. See terms and conditions for further details
Average Processing Time –
5 days working days

2. Composition & Positioning

3. Facial Features & Expression

## Overview

## Who Can Apply?
If child born overseas (Out Side India) to the Indian national, birth registration is mandatory for child. This process can be initiated along with Fresh Passport application for minor or apply separately under miscellaneous services.
Note: The applicants are advised to go through the provisions of Indian Citizenship Act, 1955 and the rules framed there under before submitting the application for grant of Indian Citizenship.

## Required Documents
##
